summary: No follow-up paper specifically addressing Conjecture 5.10 (infinitely many k-\overrightarrow{\omega}-critical tournaments for k≥3) was found. A January 2024 paper (arXiv:2401.07776) on NP-completeness of computing the clique number of tournaments explicitly disproves a conjecture of Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit and Lopes from arXiv:2310.04265, but the abstract does not identify which conjecture; full-text verification was not possible within the web-call budget. A February 2026 paper (arXiv:2602.09863) characterises tournaments with large clique number but does not appear to address k-\overrightarrow{\omega}-criticality.

since_posted.title: Computing the clique number of tournaments
since_posted.claim: Proves that deciding whether a tournament has clique number at most k is NP-complete, and provides a counterexample to a conjecture of Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit and Lopes (arXiv:2310.04265); the abstract does not specify which conjecture, so it is unclear whether this resolves Conjecture 5.10.

since_posted.title: Characterizing Large Clique Number in Tournaments
since_posted.claim: Characterises tournaments with large clique number via two families; related to the source paper's programme but does not appear to address k-\overrightarrow{\omega}-criticality or Conjecture 5.10 directly.

search_queries: k-omega-critical tournaments clique number Aboulker Aubian Charbit Lopes arXiv 2310.04265
k-critical tournament clique number infinitely many 2024 2025
notes: arXiv:2401.07776 explicitly states it disproves a conjecture of Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit and Lopes, but the abstract does not specify which one among the several conjectures in arXiv:2310.04265. Full-text access to 2401.07776 (HTML 404, PDF binary) was not achievable within the 5-call budget. Status is therefore 'unclear' rather than 'open' or 'disproved'.
